today_summary: GCM26 settled $4,157.3 with ATM IV at 21.61% and a call-heavy P/C OI ratio of 0.57 (136,770 calls vs 77,749 puts). Max pain sits at $4,780 — $623 above spot — anchored by the dominant put wall at $4,000 (4,296 contracts) and the single largest OI node still the far-OTM $5,370 call strike (8,008 contracts). Nearer-dated resistance is building in layers at $4,300/$4,400/$4,500 (4,097/4,458/4,647 contracts). Today's 1σ expected move is $56.6 (1.36%), and net GEX reads positive (+28.2) with the gamma-flip strike at $4,825.
five_day_thesis: Over the last 5 sessions (7/1–7/8) spot rallied $118.3 (+2.9%, $4,039 → $4,157) while the spot–max-pain gap narrowed from $761 to $623 as max pain drifted down modestly (-$20, $4,800 → $4,780) even as price climbed toward it. The defining structural shift is the GEX regime: dealer gamma flipped from deeply negative (-139.3) on 7/1 to positive on 7/3 and has held positive for four straight sessions (peaking +62.9 Monday, now +28.2 today) — dealers have gone from amplifying moves to dampening them. ATM IV compressed -2.56pp (24.17% → 21.61%) over the same window, consistent with a stabilizing, premium-selling environment. Put wall ($4,000) and call wall ($5,370) have not moved a single strike in 5 sessions — OI structure is frozen even as spot grinds higher into it, meaning the rally is being absorbed without fresh hedging flow at the extremes, a setup that favors continued grind over a violent break. Today's GEX reading (+28.2) is the softest of the positive stretch, worth watching for signs the stabilizing regime is fading.
what_changed: Spot +$118.3 (+2.9%) over 5 sessions, $4,039 → $4,157, narrowing the gap to max pain from $761 to $623
Net GEX flipped negative → positive on 7/3 and has held positive 4 straight days (-139.3 → +28.2), shifting dealers from vol-amplifiers to stabilizers
ATM IV compressed 24.17% → 21.61% (-2.56pp) — premium decay now favors spread sellers over long-vol positioning
Max pain drifted down $20 ($4,800 → $4,780) despite the spot rally — OI-implied pin barely moved
Put wall ($4,000, 4,296 ct) and call wall ($5,370, 8,008 ct) unchanged all 5 sessions — no new hedging flow at the extremes
P/C OI ratio steady at 0.57 — call-heavy skew persists, unwinding-vol thesis intact
prediction: Bias bullish while GCM26 holds above the $4,000 put wall and the positive-GEX regime (dealers net long gamma) persists — path of least resistance is a grind toward the $4,300–$4,500 call cluster and eventually the $4,780 max-pain / $4,825 gamma-flip zone, which sit unusually close together. A close back below $4,100 (the 1σ lower band) reopens a retest of $4,000, where negative GEX has historically accelerated declines; watch for GEX slipping back negative as the softening +28.2 reading suggests the stabilizing regime is thinning.

day_trade_cta.bias_note: 5-day uptrend (+$118.3, +2.9%) with GEX flipped positive since 7/3 and IV compressing -2.56pp; max pain sits $623 above spot, but no OI wall stands between spot and $4,300 — thin middle ground favors a grind rather than a breakout.
day_trade_cta.entry_note: Buy the pullback into the 1σ lower band ahead of the distant $4,000 put wall; avoid chasing strength with no OI resistance to lean on until $4,300.
day_trade_cta.pivot_note: 1σ upper boundary for today's session. Sustained trade above opens $4,300 then $4,500 call concentration. Rejection here fades back toward the $4,100 support test.
day_trade_cta.stop_note: -1.4% below entry midpoint, clears the 1σ lower band. A break here invalidates the bullish setup and risks a retest of the $4,000 put wall.
day_trade_cta.target_note: T1 first meaningful call cluster (4,097 ct), take 50% off. T2 next major resistance (4,647 ct) on approach to max pain — exit remaining.
day_trade_cta.risk_note: GEX +28.2, down from +62.9 Monday — dealer stabilization is easing. 1σ daily range ≈ $56.6 (1.36%). Expect choppier two-way action if GEX continues fading toward the $4,825 flip strike.
